2026-04-17T03:36:57.5116306Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T03:36:57.5116797Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T03:36:57.5117206Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T03:36:57.5117556Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:36:57.5117819Z [36;1m[0m

`sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 2404cbdff (PR #58200) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe 1.1.0 stable release of `Azure.Provisioning.Network` added significant new functionality:\\n- Expanded from 37 to 120 Network resource types\\n- Added polymorphic type support for `BaseAdminRule` (`NetworkAdminRule`, `NetworkDefaultAdminRule`), `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`, and `FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes\\n- Added support for 6 new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Empty `Authenticate the Client` section** — The heading exists in the README but has no content at all\\n- **Empty `Next steps` section** — The heading exists but contains no links or guidance\\n- **No examples for `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources** — The 6 new NSP resource types (`NetworkSecurityPerimeter`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAssociation`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLink`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLoggingConfiguration`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile`) added in 1.1.0 are undocumented in the README\\n- **No examples for polymorphic types** — The new `BaseAdminRule`/`NetworkAdminRule`/`NetworkDefaultAdminRule` hierarchy and `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`/`FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes have no README coverage despite being prominent 1.1.0 features\\n- **Thin `Key concepts` section** — With the library now covering 120 resource types, the key concepts section should enumerate major resource categories and note the polymorphic type pattern\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n1. Fill in the `Authenticate the Client` section (currently empty after the header). Add a note that this is a provisioning/infrastructure library using `DefaultAzureCredential` via `azd` and does not require client-side authentication at resource definition time, e.g.:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\nThis library is used for defining infrastructure declaratively in .NET and generating Bicep templates for deployment via [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication happens at deployment time through `azd`; no client credential is required when defining resources in code.\\n```\\n\\n2. Expand the `Key concepts` section to mention the resource count and polymorphic type pattern:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.Network` provides over 120 Network resource types including virtual networks, subnets, load balancers, firewalls, NAT gateways, network security groups, network watchers, and more.\\n\\n**Polymorphic types:** Some resources support polymorphic hierarchies. For example, `BaseAdminRule` is the base type with `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as concrete subtypes. Similarly, `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo` and `FirewallPolicyRule` follow this pattern.\\n```\\n\\n3. Add a new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` example in the `Examples` section.\\n\\n4. Add a `Next steps` content entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Provisioning libraries for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- Use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure\\n- Browse additional [Azure.Provisioning.*]((www.nuget.org/redacted) packages for other Azure services\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite sample code in a new or existing test file. The existing file to add regions to is:\\n`sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/tests/BasicNetworkTests.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate` region that demonstrates creating a `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` with at least one child resource (e.g., `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile` or `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`). Wrap any test-only setup/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic` region demonstrating defining a `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as subtypes of `BaseAdminRule`.\\n\\nThen add the corresponding README snippet placeholders in the Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Create a Network Security Perimeter\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Use polymorphic admin rules\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` currently has all required sections but two are empty:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package | ✅ |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Prerequisites | ✅ |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Too brief for v1.1.0 scope |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Missing 1.1.0 feature examples |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty |\\n| Contributing | ✅ |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n1. Fill in the `Authenticate the Client` section (currently empty after the header). Add a note that this is a provisioning/infrastructure library using `DefaultAzureCredential` via `azd` and does not require client-side authentication at resource definition time, e.g.:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\nThis library is used for defining infrastructure declaratively in .NET and generating Bicep templates for deployment via [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication happens at deployment time through `azd`; no client credential is required when defining resources in code.\\n```\\n\\n2. Expand the `Key concepts` section to mention the resource count and polymorphic type pattern:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.Network` provides over 120 Network resource types including virtual networks, subnets, load balancers, firewalls, NAT gateways, network security groups, network watchers, and more.\\n\\n**Polymorphic types:** Some resources support polymorphic hierarchies. For example, `BaseAdminRule` is the base type with `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as concrete subtypes. Similarly, `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo` and `FirewallPolicyRule` follow this pattern.\\n```\\n\\n3. Add a new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` example in the `Examples` section.\\n\\n4. Add a `Next steps` content entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Provisioning libraries for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- Use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure\\n- Browse additional [Azure.Provisioning.*]((www.nuget.org/redacted) packages for other Azure services\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite sample code in a new or existing test file. The existing file to add regions to is:\\n`sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/tests/BasicNetworkTests.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate` region that demonstrates creating a `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` with at least one child resource (e.g., `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile` or `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`). Wrap any test-only setup/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic` region demonstrating defining a `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as subtypes of `BaseAdminRule`.\\n\\nThen add the corresponding README snippet placeholders in the Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Create a Network Security Perimeter\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Use polymorphic admin rules\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` currently has all required sections but two are empty:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package | ✅ |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Prerequisites | ✅ |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Too brief for v1.1.0 scope |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Missing 1.1.0 feature examples |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty |\\n| Contributing | ✅ |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order.
